description_long: This bungalow features a two-story structure with a complex, intersecting roof design, primarily composed of dark brown, steeply pitched gables and ridges. The exterior walls are a light beige color, accented by dark brown trim around the windows, doors, and roof edges. Multiple sets of rectangular windows with arched tops are symmetrically distributed across all visible facades, each window glowing warmly as if illuminated from within. The main entrance is centrally located beneath a prominent gabled section, with a dark wooden door and a small overhang. The building's layout includes a main rectangular volume with two perpendicular wings, creating an L-shaped footprint. The overall appearance suggests a traditional, storybook-style residential building, suitable for a quaint urban or suburban neighborhood.
description: A two-story, beige and brown bungalow with intersecting gabled roofs, symmetrical arched windows, and a central front entrance, exuding a traditional, storybook charm.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance beneath a large gable, with symmetrical windows and the intersecting roof lines clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the elongated wing and the curvature of the main roof, with multiple windows along the facade.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the main structure and the perpendicular wing, with consistent window placement and roof detailing.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the secondary wing and the continuation of the gabled roof, with arched windows and brown trim.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical front-facing windows with evenly spaced side windows
